title: More media like End Of Evangelion
display_title: More media like End Of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Losernextdoor » Fri Dec 27, 2024 8:27 pm Okay so, End Of Evangelion is one of my favorite movies of all times and I haven't found anything quite like it since watching it. Please please please do you have any recommendations to scratch this itch in my brain.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by ::KL7:: » Sat Dec 28, 2024 12:53 am Off the top of my head, Madoka Magika Rebellion left me as depressed as EOE did if that counts?

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Dec 28, 2024 11:22 am ::KL7:: wrote: Off the top of my head, Madoka Magika Rebellion left me as depressed as EOE did if that counts?

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sat Dec 28, 2024 4:29 pm Madoka is like 12 episodes long, its not a exactly huge time commitment.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by ::KL7:: » Sat Dec 28, 2024 5:22 pm That is true. I did mean to add I was super invested in the anime series. Watch the anime, then the film.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun Dec 29, 2024 5:45 am Space Runaway Ideon: Be Invoked , obviously. That's where EoE got a lot of its crazy from. Of course, as with Evangelion and Madoka, for optimal viewing experience you need to watch the preceding TV series first.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Mon Dec 30, 2024 12:18 pm A comparison I see a lot is Twin Peaks and its film counterpart Fire Walk With Me. It might seem like an odd comparison, but there's some connecting points--TP was popular in Japan, and its film actually performed better and was more well-received there by a large degree. It's influential enough in the anime world that Anno was asked about it once (IIRC, he hadn't seen it at the time), and is referenced often, including being the main inspiration for Link's Awakening . One big difference is that while Eva is great the entire way through and progressively steepens its tone and stakes, there's a notoriously bad stretch of Twin Peaks in the back half of its second season owing to network meddling and the creators briefly stepping away, and that stretch is plagued by aimlessness and lack of stakes. But the similarities are there in that watching NGE and then EoE is a very specific experience, a series capped by something that transcends everything prior in a huge affective leap, and I'd say the same is true for TP the more you watch. These comparisons were made before a sequel series to TP and its film existed, which is, like, that same kind of ramping up for 18 hours straight. Imagine if instead of rebooting, EoE just kept on going. TP is my main love next to Eva, so I can't recommend it enough, but it's not to everyone's taste. If Eva benefits from foreknowledge of Mecha and sci-fi, TP is a bit of a mish-mash of mystery tropes, noir, the kudzu plot of procedurals and soap operas and, really, 50s-90s TV in general. But like Eva, it has moments of high art, dips into horror, gets esoteric, and can become sublime.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Tue Dec 31, 2024 12:54 am Honestly I don't think the "bad stretch" of Twin Peaks S2 is even all that bad (It's just not Lynch good, and well few people alive can even match him at the height of his powers anyways), and a fair amount of that section of the show actually gets reflected/refracted in The Return in...strange ways.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 1:19 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Honestly I don't think the "bad stretch" of Twin Peaks S2 is even all that bad (It's just not Lynch good, and well few people alive can even match him at the height of his powers anyways), and a fair amount of that section of the show actually gets reflected/refracted in The Return in...strange ways.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Tue Dec 31, 2024 2:30 am Axx°N N. wrote: But while I find new things to appreciate each time, I'm also always finding new things to depreciate. This time I noticed that there's a lot of weirdly subpar staging, as in how scenes are coordinated so that characters are framed well and maximally audible. Lots of muffled dialogue to go with the general down-trend in directing strength, a general lack of attention to detail for a spurt.

[]: Re: More media like End Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Dec 31, 2024 2:59 am The Killer of Heroes wrote: Hmm, I'll have to keep this in mind for my next rewatch. I mean I have zero doubt Lynch is substantially better at this kind of basic stuff than the ragtag crew that had to sail the ship on their own for a while, but I don't remember any particularly bad muffled dialogue or anything like that during their stretch. It has been years since my last watch though.

Please please please do you have any recommendations to scratch this itch in my brain.

It's influential enough in the anime world that Anno was asked about it once (IIRC, he hadn't seen it at the time), and is referenced often, including being the main inspiration for Link's Awakening . Imagine if instead of rebooting, EoE just kept on going. TP is my main love next to Eva, so I can't recommend it enough, but it's not to everyone's taste. But like Eva, it has moments of high art, dips into horror, gets esoteric, and can become sublime.
